title: snappy winey algerian post-workout dish tagine
description: a flavorful and nutritious post-workout tagine featuring coconut cream and a sweet glaze, inspired by algerian cuisine
instructions: heat the coconut oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
add the onions and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
add the garlic and ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
add the red wine and bring to a boil.
add the coconut cream, honey, cumin, coriander, paprika, salt, and black pepper. stir to combine.
add the chicken (or plant-based alternative) and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
transfer the saucepan to the oven and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through.
meanwhile, toast the almonds in a small skillet over medium heat, stirring frequently, until fragrant and lightly browned.
add the flaxseeds to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
remove the saucepan from the oven and stir in the toasted almonds, flaxseeds, and chopped fresh herbs.
serve the tagine hot, garnished with additional fresh herbs and a drizzle of red wine glaze, if desired.
to make the red wine glaze, combine 2 tablespoons of red wine with 1 tablespoon of maple syrup in a small bowl. whisk until smooth and reserve.
to make the snappy winey glaze, combine 2 tablespoons of red wine with 1 tablespoon of lemon juice and 1 tablespoon of honey in a small bowl. whisk until smooth and reserve.
serve the tagine with a side of salted water for added flavor and hydration.
